name: Analog Horror PSA
description: Fake government-issued emergency behavioral PSA in analog-horror style. Cold robo-female ElevenLabs VO narrates an IF / DO-NOT / BUT / AND beat scaffold over flat yellow pixelated 1970s-civil-defense pictogram icons on pure-black bg. 30s, 9:16, ~10 scenes x ~3s. No music by default — pure SFX bed (VHS hiss, CRT pop, EBS alert, signal-lost 1kHz). VT323 word-pop captions with red glitch shadow. Heavy 5-layer chromatic aberration on icons AND captions synchronized to a single jitter beat. Climax cuts to full-screen SMPTE-style blurred color bars + signal-lost tone. Format owes its DNA to Father-Eats-in-the-Basement-class EBS PSA found-footage videos.

stackSummary.keyframes: google/gemini-3-pro-image-preview (nano-banana-pro) via OpenRouter — flat yellow #ffd400 pictograms on pure black #000000. ONE prototype shot first (gpt-5.4-image-2 OR nano-banana), then style-lock and batch the remaining ~9 sibling icons sequentially with that prototype as --ref. NEVER use --queue (hits an undocumented OpenRouter burst-cap 403 on parallel image gen). Pure black bg is mandatory — chroma-keyed to alpha via ffmpeg colorkey before composition.
stackSummary.i2v: OFF. This format is icon-driven, not photoreal-video-driven. All motion is Remotion-side (eye-dot blinks, slash flickers, jitter, glitch beats). Adding kling/seedance i2v breaks the PSA-graphic register.
stackSummary.voiceover: ElevenLabs eleven_multilingual_v2 — cold robo-female PSA monotone. Voice {{vo_voice_id}} (project used 'Alerter' community voice + 'weA4Q36twV5kwSaTEL0Q' Ava as fallback). Input text in ALL CAPS to coerce flatter cadence. stability ~0.5, similarity ~0.75, style 0.10-0.15. ~250ms pause between sentences. Verify voice exists via /v1/voices/<id> before bulk gen (community voice IDs can 404 between sessions). Run sequentially, ElevenLabs 429s on >3 concurrent.
stackSummary.sfx: ElevenLabs sound_generation_v2 via `ralphy generate sfx`. ~13-17 clips: VHS-hiss bed, static-pop (per scene start), EBS alert tone, low sub-bass drone, dog-breath, mirror-shimmer, buried child-whisper, low-growl-rise, signal-lost 1kHz sine, RGB-static burst, wind-through-trees, collar-jingle-stops, distant-bark-slowed. For climax monster reveals: generate 4 growl VARIANTS and LAYER at staggered offsets — single growl reads as 'chihuahua yipping'.
stackSummary.music: OFF BY DEFAULT — matches reference. Optional faint sub-bass synth-tension drone at -34 LUFS (yt-dlp pull from a user-supplied YT track is the 2-min path; don't bother with ElevenLabs Music for this format). If used, volume 0.35-0.40 to sit above the VHS-hiss bed.
stackSummary.captions: VT323 word-pop, yellow #ffd400 fill + 6px black stroke, 112px, bottom-center y=1500, per-word fire, 350ms hold, 60ms fade, red #ff0033 glitch offset 4px. SAME 5-layer chromatic split + jitter beat as icons. Captions baked from the VO text uppercase.
stackSummary.render: Remotion 4.0.441. CRF 30 + `-tune grain` is the right preset for noise-heavy renders (default CRF 18 produces 190MB / 30s files; CRF 30 + grain produces 28MB with zero perceptual loss because human eyes don't track snow pixels).

constants.beatScaffold: HOOK / SETUP / IF x3 / DO-NOT x3 / BUT-reversal / AND-climax-sting
constants.iconStyleLock: flat-yellow-#ffd400 on pure-black-#000000, stencil-wobble edges, NO outline, NO internal texture, NO anti-aliasing-feel-but-not-pixel-art, 55-60pct frame, centered, single-subject pictogram
constants.chromaSplit: 5-layer RGB ghosts synchronized on ONE jitter beat across icons + captions
constants.climaxVisual: blurred-SMPTE-color-bars (NEVER random confetti dots) + signal-lost-1kHz hold last 1.5s

beatStructure.purpose: EBS-style title card — red {{subject_warning_id}} characters + horizontal red bars. Instant tension.
Frame the PSA in domestic-{{subject_warning}} context. Yellow pictogram of the un-trusted entity in its calm/ordinary pose.
beatStructure.vo: This is a residential behavioral alert.

beatStructure.purpose: IF tell #1 — first oddly-precise behavior the entity exhibits.
IF tell #2 — escalates from #1; ideally something that breaks physics or reality slightly (reflection-doesn't-blink class).
IF tell #3 — strongest reality-break of the three (whispers-your-name class).
DO-NOT #1 — first behavioral instruction. Add a red prohibition slash to the pictogram.
DO-NOT #2 — second instruction. Ideally callbacks the IF tells.
DO-NOT #3 — third instruction. Should callback IF-1 (the setup ordinary behavior is now forbidden).
BUT — the dread payoff. Reveal that the {{subject_warning}} the viewer trusts is NOT what it appears. Icon: empty-collar / headstone / something-that-marks-an-absence.
AND — final sting. Icon distorts (mouth-too-wide, eyes-go-void), HARD CUT to blurred SMPTE color bars + signal-lost 1kHz hold for last 1.5s.

slots.description: Org issuing the PSA. Default literal: an in-universe agency name like 'Emergency Broadcast System' / 'Residential Compliance Authority' / 'Office of Domestic Vigilance'. Keep the in-universe register — a real consumer brand placed here ruins the PSA illusion.

What entity the viewer is being told NOT to trust. Source project used 'your dog'. Other valid examples: 'your child', 'your reflection', 'your neighbor', 'the man in the bedroom', 'the second moon'. The entity should be something the viewer takes for granted in their domestic life.
Short alphanumeric bulletin/section code shown in the red EBS-style hook card and named in the VO. 1-3 characters max so it reads at hook size. Source: '9-D'.
Spoken form of {{subject_warning_id}} for VO. Source: 'nine D' for '9-D'.
First oddly-precise behavior the entity exhibits. Domestic + slightly off. Source: 'watches you sleep'. Keep the verb concrete, the time/place explicit.
Second tell — should break physics or reality slightly. Source: 'reflection does not blink'. Mirror-class beats work especially well.
Third tell — strongest reality-break, ideally tied to the viewer personally. Source: 'whispers your name when you are alone'.
slots.defaultExample: whispers your name when you are alone

slots.description: First behavioral instruction. Tied to a specific time / context. Source: 'look in its eyes after sunset'.
slots.defaultExample: look in its eyes after sunset

slots.description: Second instruction. Source: 'say its old name'. Tying the instruction to a name / identity object adds dread.
Third instruction — should callback {{tell_1}} or {{setup}} (the ordinary behavior is now forbidden). Source: 'let it on your bed' callbacks the IF-1 'watches you sleep'.
The dread payoff — reveals the {{subject_warning}} the viewer trusts is not what it appears. Source: 'your dog died three years ago'. Should be flat, declarative, past-tense.
slots.defaultExample: your dog died three years ago

slots.description: Final sting line. Should be ominous-present-tense + future-implication. Source: 'tonight it stops pretending'. The mp4 cuts to color-bar static immediately after this line.

ElevenLabs voice ID for the cold robo-female PSA. Project winner was 'Alerter' community voice generated manually in 11Labs UI. Fallback API-callable voice: 'weA4Q36twV5kwSaTEL0Q' (Ava). For non-English (Russian / etc.) consider 'eleven_v3' model + manual UI tuning since multilingual_v2 inflects too human.
Spoken language of the VO. Source: 'en'. For non-English, EXPLICITLY pick a voice that handles that language flat — ElevenLabs default English voices added accent slip on Russian in past projects.
Distribution intent — TikTok-en-US is the default. Some PSAs work cross-language (visuals + minimal VO) but the cold-robo-female cadence is heavily English-coded.

scene-01-icon-ebs.purpose: Style anchor — red EBS-style title-card pictogram. Use as --ref for any new hook-card icon you generate (different {{subject_warning_id}}).

scene-02-icon-dog-silhouette.purpose: Master style prototype — the v10 winner. THIS is the --ref to lock the yellow-pictogram style on the other 9 sibling icons. Use it even if the subject is no longer a dog: the style is what carries (flat yellow, pure black, stencil-wobble edges, no outline).

scene-03-icon-dog-bed-watcher.purpose: Example icon for {{tell_1}} class (entity-watches-sleeper). Compositional cue: split-frame with both subject and victim in the pictogram.

scene-04-icon-dog-mirror.purpose: Example icon for {{tell_2}} class (reflection-doesn't-match). Compositional cue: subject + mirror frame, mirror version has wrong-eye-marks.

scene-05-icon-dog-whisper.purpose: Example icon for {{tell_3}} class (entity-whispers / scrambled-speech). Compositional cue: speech bubble with non-letter glyphs (╳ ▒ ╳).

scene-06-icon-no-eye-contact.purpose: Example icon for {{do_not_1}} class. Compositional cue: subject front-view + red diagonal prohibition slash. The red slash is the DO-NOT marker.

scene-07-icon-no-old-name.purpose: Example icon for {{do_not_2}} class (identity-object + slash). Compositional cue: small object that names the entity (name-tag, photo, doorplate) + prohibition slash.

scene-08-icon-no-bed.purpose: Example icon for {{do_not_3}} class. Compositional cue: callback to setup's location (bed in source) + entity mid-action + red slash.

scene-09-icon-empty-collar.purpose: Example icon for {{but_reveal}} class — marks an absence. Compositional cue: object that belonged to entity + date / headstone / empty location.

scene-10-icon-dog-grin.purpose: Example icon for {{and_sting}} climax. Compositional cue: entity's face goes wrong (mouth-too-wide, eyes-pure-void). Only visible for first 0.8s before HARD CUT to color-bar static.

doNotCopyLiterally: {{subject_warning}}, {{tell_*}}, {{do_not_*}}, {{but_reveal}}, {{and_sting}}, {{vo_voice_id}}, exact prompts. Vibe reference only. The beat scaffold (HOOK / SETUP / IF x3 / DO-NOT x3 / BUT / AND), the visual register (yellow pictogram on black + VT323 captions + 5-layer chromatic split + scanline/noise overlay + signal-lost color-bar climax), and the audio policy (cold robo-female + no music + per-scene static pop) ARE the template — keep them literal.
referenceNotes: User reference not required when {{subject_warning}} is a generic domestic noun ('your dog', 'your child', 'your reflection'). However, AGENTS.md hard rule #3 applies: if the brief names a real person, brand, or specific real entity as the un-trusted thing, require a reference. The format CAN be subverted into branded content (e.g. 'your <product> is not your <product>') but only with the user's explicit override — the PSA illusion depends on the un-trusted thing reading as generic-domestic.
compositionTemplateNote: Source project's Remotion lives at src/videos/analog-horror-fridge-001/ (NOT src/lib/templates/) — per kind-decision.md flowchart, kind=vibe-style. Consumer needs to hand-author the Remotion composition, OR copy and rename src/videos/analog-horror-fridge-001/index.tsx as a starting point. The five reusable overlay primitives (MobiusWobble / SnowCanvas / VcrTrackingCanvas / MobiusScanlines / GlitchCaption / GlitchXIcon / SignalLostColorBars) are slated to move to src/lib/components/overlays/AnalogTV.tsx per POSTMORTEM.md open issue #7.
